session_hint: the TLS handshake failure investigation on the API gateway
question: What did we rule out when investigating TLS handshake failures on the API gateway?
turns: About 2% of HTTPS connections to our API gateway fail with SSL_ERROR_RX_RECORD_TOO_LONG. It affects older Android clients specifically. First thought: the certificate chain is incomplete and clients can't build trust.

Ran ssllabs.com scan and openssl s_client -connect: the full chain including the intermediate certificate is served correctly and trusted by all major root stores. Chain completeness is not the issue.

Checked certificate expiry and the Subject Alternative Name field. Certificate is valid for 8 more months and the SAN covers both the apex domain and www subdomain. Certificate validity and SAN coverage are correct, ruling those out.

Root cause: the gateway is configured for TLSv1.3 only but the affected Android 6 and 7 clients only support up to TLSv1.2. No mutually supported protocol version exists so the handshake fails immediately. Added TLSv1.2 to the accepted protocol list and the affected clients connected successfully.
